notes: Latest mppx contains hardening and x402/EVM surface changes, but no one-time Tempo refund API for WooCommerce order refunds.
Tempo session-channel close receipts can report refundedToPayer for unused channel deposits; that is not the same as refunding a completed one-time shop order.

stripe_link_cli.scope: Buyer-side Stripe Shared Payment Token CLI; not a merchant-side Tempo refund executor.

recommended_first_staging_shop.reason: Tempo's current test path is USD-stablecoin oriented; a USD WooCommerce shop avoids claiming EUR settlement from a pathUSD proof.

eur_stablecoin_candidates.preferred_role: First EUR x402 candidate when the selected facilitator supports EURC on the target EVM network.
eur_stablecoin_candidates.authorization_shape: EIP-3009 where supported; otherwise Permit2/generic ERC-20 flow.
requires: Facilitator support for the selected chain and EURC token address.
Quote-bound verifier evidence for amount, token, network, payTo, quote hash, payment contract hash, and transaction reference.
A rail-specific refund verifier that proves the EURC refund transfer back to the approved recipient.

eur_stablecoin_candidates.preferred_role: EU-regulated EUR settlement candidate when x402 facilitator/token support is available or custom facilitator support is added.
eur_stablecoin_candidates.authorization_shape: ERC-20 with ERC-2612 Permit/ERC-1271; use Permit2 or a custom x402 facilitator when EIP-3009 is unavailable.
requires: Monerium onboarding/accounting path for merchant settlement and redemption.
Facilitator or custom verifier support for EURe on the selected network.
A rail-specific refund verifier that proves the EURe refund transfer back to the approved recipient.

non_goals: Do not claim EUR settlement or EUR refund execution from a Tempo pathUSD proof.
Do not mark WooCommerce refunds real from mppx CLI success alone.
Do not treat Stripe Link CLI updates as Tempo refund support.
